purpose: Prevent visual blind spots by declaring which stable system modules require Canvas views, which modules are covered by shared views, and which modules are explicitly deferred or not applicable.

policy.blind_spot_definition: A module, workflow, registry, loop, schema, tool, taxonomy or governance surface is a visual blind spot when it affects system understanding or operation but has neither a required Canvas view nor an explicit visual decision with reason and review trigger.
policy.no_implicit_status_rule: Every module listed in system/system-closure-map.json must have exactly one module_decision here.
required_when: The surface is a stable system governance module.
The surface explains architecture, route, dataflow, workflow, loop state, diagnostic repair, schema contract, tool runtime or closure status.
The surface is a knowledge routing structure used by machine-readable fields such as primary_level_3.
The surface is repeatedly inspected by humans to understand or operate the knowledge-base system.
policy.validation_gate: scripts/validate-system.ps1 must fail when a closure module lacks a visual decision, when a required view is missing from canvas-registry, or when a closed/shared visual projection references a Canvas path that is not covered here.

required_views.coverage: Top-level visual entrypoint for current Canvas views.
Shared system overview for module boundaries, major dataflow, route-facing governance and early shared visual coverage.
Dedicated Canvas subsystem architecture, visual policy, view families, sync triggers and quality gates.
Dedicated Canvas directory, reading-order and lifecycle boundary map.
High-level knowledge-domain taxonomy overview and taxonomy-refinement loop status as a related domain concern.
Complete level-2 and level-3 taxonomy matrix required for primary_level_3 routing inspection.

Dedicated diagnostic workflow, layer diagnosis matrix, controlled failure-learning loop, positive-model-first issue contract, optional guardrail fields, repair routing, visual projection sync and diagnosis acceptance architecture.
Dedicated system-closure matrix, module maturity and closure quality-gate visualization.
Dedicated review-system architecture, review classes, Canvas visual aesthetic review, finding classification, external knowledge review boundary and handoff routing.
Dedicated Canvas for system module topology, branch decisions and relationship governance.
Dedicated architecture view for machine-readable information-map manifest, shard, root map and validation surfaces.
System-level loop orchestration architecture, durable loop registry, state lifecycle, managed loops, handoff intake and legacy taxonomy-refinement adoption.
Dedicated architecture view for the knowledge ingestion subsystem, intake modes, source-quality gates, legacy asset adoption and handoffs into domain/review/diagnostics/loop/index surfaces.
Dedicated architecture view for Git local version governance, remote origin contract, validation, sensitive-surface boundary and remote action approval gate.

module_decisions.reason: The inbox is temporary raw capture storage and is not a stable structure yet.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create a dedicated intake visual view if inbox gains a queue schema, triage workflow or recurring quality gate.

module_decisions.reason: Source registration is currently represented by source files and catalog indexing; it has no dedicated registry graph yet.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create a source-registry view if source metadata becomes a dedicated JSON registry or source quality workflows become multi-step.

module_decisions.reason: 项目根定位是读图口径和系统总览口径，不需要独立大图；入口图和系统总览必须共同暴露“无界知识是知识工程系统”的定位。
module_decisions.review_trigger: 当项目定位、Codex workflow 双重角色、系统/知识内容关系或 L0/L1 读图口径变化时，审查这两个共享视图。

module_decisions.reason: Domain taxonomy is a stable machine routing structure and level-3 classification must be inspectable visually.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Update required views whenever level-1, level-2 or level-3 taxonomy changes.

module_decisions.reason: The Canvas subsystem owns the visual architecture surface and must expose entry, overview, subsystem and placement governance views.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Add or update required views whenever a new visual family, Canvas directory layer or visual quality gate becomes stable.

module_decisions.reason: Module governance is currently visualized through the system overview because it governs cross-module placement instead of an internal workflow.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create a dedicated module-governance Canvas view if the policy grows new state, subagents, schemas or multi-step intake.

module_decisions.reason: Closure status is a cross-module governance matrix and must remain visually inspectable.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Update the closure view whenever closure facets, module statuses or visual coverage gates change.

module_decisions.reason: Technical documentation is a routed docs module today; it does not yet have CI, release or operations workflows that need a dedicated Canvas view.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create a technical-operations view when release, backup, CI, scheduled validation or GitHub publishing becomes an executable workflow.

module_decisions.reason: Diagnosis has workflow stages, repair classes, layer diagnosis matrix, failure-learning loop, queue schema and acceptance gates that need a dedicated visual map.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Update the diagnostic view whenever repair classes, issue schema, layer matrix, failure-learning policy, quality gates, visual projection rules or orchestration rules change.

module_decisions.reason: Artifact-form choice is currently a shared governance decision visible from the system overview and tool map.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create a dedicated artifact-form view if decisions become a reusable workflow with state, examples or multiple runtime branches.

module_decisions.reason: loop-orchestration is a system-governance module for durable cross-module loops, not only a taxonomy status block; it needs a dedicated view to prevent future loop/state/workflow visual blind spots.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Review whenever a new durable loop is registered, loop runner automation is introduced, or loop handoff rules change.

module_decisions.reason: Atomic notes are still content entries rather than a stable retrieval or quality workflow.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create an atomic-knowledge view when note retrieval topology, quality gates or concept graph structure becomes stable.

module_decisions.reason: Synthesis outputs are early content artifacts without a dedicated production workflow.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create a synthesis workflow view when multi-source briefs become frequent or synthesis quality gates become durable.

module_decisions.reason: Codex workflow consumption is represented in the system overview while workflow packs are still early.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create a dedicated workflow view when a workflow spans three or more stable steps or a project-specific context pack becomes reusable.

module_decisions.reason: Eval cases exist, but no automated eval runner or multi-stage eval workflow is stable yet.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create an eval view when retrieval, taxonomy or workflow evaluation becomes a recurring executable gate.

module_decisions.reason: Indexing is currently a simple generated catalog rather than a multi-index architecture.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create an index architecture view when SQLite FTS, vector index, graph index or multiple export pipelines become active.

module_decisions.reason: Schemas are currently small shared contracts referenced from the system overview and validators.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create a schema-contract view when schema consumers, migrations or cross-schema dependencies become complex.

module_decisions.reason: Tool runtime decisions are currently visible from the system overview and tool-map registry.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Create a tool-runtime view when scripts grow beyond validation/indexing or gain service-like runtime decisions.

module_decisions.reason: Review is a stable upstream module for system, external knowledge and Canvas visual aesthetic quality inspection, and its handoff boundary with diagnostics must be visually inspectable.
module_decisions.review_trigger: Update the review-system view whenever review classes, specialized review modules, criteria sets, handoff targets, review record contract or validator behavior changes.

module_decisions.reason: 系统结构治理是新增稳定核心治理模块，负责新模块归属和分支决策，必须有专属可视化入口。
module_decisions.review_trigger: 当 module-relationship-map、branch_decision_policy 或 module-layer-model 派生逻辑变化时，审查并更新该专属 Canvas。

module_decisions.reason: 系统信息地图是稳定核心治理模块，负责系统机器字典、轻量读取入口和引用面索引，必须有专属可视化入口。
module_decisions.review_trigger: 当 information-map、manifest、shard、schema、route 或引用面同步策略变化时，审查并更新该专属 Canvas。

module_decisions.reason: 知识进入方式是长期稳定、跨模块的系统能力，必须有 dedicated 视觉影子，避免只在 source-registry 或系统总览阶段块里隐形。
module_decisions.review_trigger: 当新增来源类型、摄入模板、来源质量门、领域路由交接、review/diagnostics/loop handoff 或 source-registry 结构变化时，审查此图。

module_decisions.reason: Git 版本治理是长期稳定的系统治理能力，影响所有文件的版本、发布和远端动作边界，必须有 dedicated 视觉影子。
module_decisions.review_trigger: 当 remote、branch policy、提交策略、版本周期、发布策略、CI、敏感信息边界或 GitHub 操作权限变化时，审查此图。
